source_observations.local_summary: ARC Prize 2026 lists $2M in prizes across three tracks: ARC-AGI-3, ARC-AGI-2, and Paper Prize. Key dates include March 25 start, June 30 ARC-AGI-3 Milestone #1, September 30 Milestone #2, November 2 submissions due, November 8 papers due, and December 4 results.
source_observations.route_effect: High upside and strategic learning value, but the route is not quick cash and requires rules, Kaggle, open-source, and submission gates before external action.

source_observations.local_summary: ARC-AGI-3 challenges participants to build AI agents for novel interactive environments without instructions. The track lists $850K total, including a $700K grand prize for 100%, guaranteed top-score awards, and milestone prizes. Submissions must go through Kaggle, no internet access is available during evaluation, and all code/methods must be open sourced to be prize eligible.
source_observations.route_effect: Any serious baseline must be offline-capable, reproducible, open-source-ready, and tested locally before account/rules/submission gates.

source_observations.local_summary: ARC-AGI-3 docs describe an interactive benchmark for exploration, percept-plan-action, memory, goal acquisition, and alignment. Quickstart references installing the arc-agi toolkit and optional API key, then playing public environments.
source_observations.route_effect: Toolkit/API use remains gated here; this packet keeps the baseline at synthetic/local proof until approval exists.

source_observations.local_summary: Existing local toy harness checked five synthetic tasks and passed all five: translation, recolor, mirror, crop-and-tile, and explicit no-solution failure case. It used no official ARC data, no Kaggle login/download, no paid compute/API, no submission/public action, and no external side effects.
source_observations.route_effect: The local kill criterion has enough proof to justify a next local baseline design, but not enough to justify Kaggle/account/submission action.

baseline_tracks.local_start: Extend synthetic toy harness from static transforms into a tiny turn-based environment with replay traces.
baseline_tracks.why: The official track is agentic and offline-evaluated, so the baseline must focus on exploration, memory, world-model inference, and action planning without API calls.
deliverables: Offline local environment fixture with at least three tiny games.
Replay JSON with observations, actions, score, and terminal state.
Failure taxonomy for exploration, goal inference, and planning misses.

baseline_tracks.local_start: Continue the existing DSL harness and add more transform families before using any official/gated competition data.
baseline_tracks.why: Existing synthetic grid-transform proof already works locally, and static tasks are a cleaner proving ground for deterministic DSL search.
deliverables: At least eight synthetic transform families.

baseline_tracks.local_start: Maintain a structured experiment log from every toy-harness and baseline run.
baseline_tracks.why: Paper route may preserve value even if leaderboard performance is weak, but claims need reproducible experiments.
deliverables: Paper outline after at least two local baselines.

success_criteria: Create three toy interactive environments with deterministic state transitions.
Run at least two baseline policies: random and simple exploration-memory.
Write replay JSON for every run.
Pass deterministic replay validation without network, API, Kaggle, official data, paid compute, or account access.
next_local_sprint.kill_or_continue_rule: Continue toward ARC-AGI-3 only if the simple exploration-memory baseline beats random on at least two of three toy environments and the replay logs identify actionable failure modes.

why: ARC Prize 2026 has large upside and strong strategic fit, and the local toy harness already proves a small deterministic baseline path. External action remains premature because Kaggle/rules/submission/open-source/license/compute gates are unresolved and no official baseline has been run.
acceptance_checks: Official ARC Prize 2026 overview, ARC-AGI-3 track, and docs are summarized.
Local toy harness evidence is incorporated.
At least three baseline tracks are defined with deliverables and gates.
A next local sprint is specified with success criteria and a measurable continue/kill rule.
No Kaggle account/login, rules acceptance, data download, submission, paid compute, API key, worker/runtime start, public action, or external side effect occurs.
next_action: Create the ARC-AGI-3 micro-environment replay baseline locally; keep Kaggle account/rules, official data, submission, paper/public action, paid compute/API, worker/runtime, and external side effects blocked until human approval.
